name: AWS Services
description: AWS service names, descriptions, use cases, key facts, and exam tips

cards.back: Elastic Compute Cloud — resizable virtual servers in the cloud. Supports On-Demand, Reserved, Spot, and Dedicated instances.
Unused EC2 capacity at up to 90% discount. Can be interrupted with 2-minute notice. Ideal for fault-tolerant, flexible workloads.
Simple Storage Service — object storage with 99.999999999% (11 nines) durability. Stores unlimited data as objects in buckets.
Standard, Intelligent-Tiering, Standard-IA, One Zone-IA, Glacier Instant Retrieval, Glacier Flexible, Glacier Deep Archive.
Relational Database Service — managed databases supporting MySQL, PostgreSQL, MariaDB, Oracle, SQL Server, and Aurora.
AWS's cloud-native relational database. MySQL/PostgreSQL compatible. Up to 5x faster than MySQL, 3x faster than PostgreSQL. Auto-scales storage up to 128 TB.
Serverless compute — run code without provisioning servers. Pay per request and compute time. Max execution time: 15 minutes.
Fully managed NoSQL key-value and document database. Single-digit millisecond latency at any scale. Supports both document and key-value models.
Virtual Private Cloud — logically isolated network in AWS. You control IP ranges, subnets, route tables, and gateways.
A range of IP addresses in your VPC. Public subnets have routes to an Internet Gateway; private subnets do not.
A horizontally scaled, redundant VPC component that allows communication between your VPC and the internet.
Allows instances in private subnets to access the internet while preventing inbound connections from the internet. Managed, scales automatically.
Global Content Delivery Network (CDN). Caches content at 400+ edge locations. Integrates with S3, EC2, ALB, and Lambda@Edge.
Scalable DNS web service. Supports domain registration, DNS routing, and health checking. Named after TCP/UDP port 53.
Simple, Weighted, Latency-based, Failover, Geolocation, Geoproximity, and Multi-value answer routing.
Distributes incoming traffic across multiple targets. Types: Application LB (Layer 7), Network LB (Layer 4), Gateway LB, Classic LB (legacy).

cards.front: What is the difference between ALB and NLB?
cards.back: ALB operates at Layer 7 (HTTP/HTTPS), supports path/host routing. NLB operates at Layer 4 (TCP/UDP), handles millions of requests/sec with ultra-low latency.
Elastic Container Service — fully managed container orchestration. Supports Docker containers. Can run on EC2 or Fargate.
Serverless compute for containers. No need to manage EC2 instances. Works with both ECS and EKS. Pay for vCPU and memory used.
Elastic Kubernetes Service — managed Kubernetes control plane. Runs standard Kubernetes so you can use existing tools and plugins.
Simple Queue Service — fully managed message queuing. Supports Standard (at-least-once, best-effort ordering) and FIFO (exactly-once, ordered) queues.
Simple Notification Service — pub/sub messaging for A2A and A2P. Push-based delivery to SQS, Lambda, HTTP, email, SMS, and mobile.

cards.front: What is the difference between SQS and SNS?
cards.back: SQS: pull-based queue, consumer polls. SNS: push-based pub/sub, publisher pushes to subscribers. Often used together for fan-out pattern.
Identity and Access Management — controls who (authentication) can do what (authorization) in AWS. Users, groups, roles, and policies.
An identity with permissions policies that can be assumed by AWS services, users, or applications. No long-term credentials — uses temporary security tokens.
A JSON document defining permissions. Contains Effect (Allow/Deny), Action, Resource, and optional Condition. Deny always overrides Allow.
Infrastructure as Code (IaC) service. Define resources in JSON/YAML templates. Creates stacks of resources that can be updated or deleted together.
Monitoring and observability service. Collects metrics, logs, and events. Set alarms, create dashboards, and trigger automated actions.
Records API calls and account activity. Provides governance, compliance, and audit trail. Logs who did what, when, and from where.
Managed in-memory caching. Supports Redis and Memcached. Microsecond latency for read-heavy workloads. Use for session stores, leaderboards, caching.
Real-time streaming data platform. Services: Data Streams, Data Firehose, Data Analytics, Video Streams. Process streaming data at scale.
Automatically adjusts capacity to maintain performance at lowest cost. Supports EC2, ECS, DynamoDB, Aurora. Uses scaling policies (target tracking, step, scheduled).
Fully managed petabyte-scale data warehouse. Columnar storage, massively parallel processing (MPP). Up to 10x better performance than traditional warehouses.
Serverless ETL (Extract, Transform, Load) service. Auto-discovers data schemas with crawlers. Maintains a Data Catalog for analytics.
Serverless interactive query service. Analyze data in S3 using standard SQL. Pay per query. No infrastructure to manage. Uses Presto engine.
Elastic Block Store — persistent block storage for EC2. Types: gp3/gp2 (general), io2/io1 (provisioned IOPS), st1 (throughput), sc1 (cold). Snapshots to S3.
Elastic File System — managed NFS file system. Shared across multiple EC2 instances. Auto-scales. Supports Linux workloads. Multi-AZ by default.
Dedicated private network connection from on-premises to AWS. Bypasses internet. Consistent latency, higher throughput. 1 Gbps or 10 Gbps connections.
Encrypted connection over the internet between your network and AWS. Site-to-Site VPN (IPsec) or Client VPN. Quick to set up vs Direct Connect.
Managed service to create, publish, and manage APIs at any scale. Supports REST, HTTP, and WebSocket APIs. Handles throttling, auth, and caching.
Serverless orchestration service for workflows. Coordinate Lambda functions and AWS services using visual state machines. Handles retries and error handling.
User authentication and authorization. User Pools: sign-up/sign-in. Identity Pools: grant temporary AWS credentials. Supports social/SAML/OIDC federation.
Key Management Service — create and manage encryption keys. Integrates with most AWS services. Supports symmetric (AES-256) and asymmetric keys (RSA, ECC).
Securely store and rotate secrets (database passwords, API keys). Automatic rotation with Lambda. Pay per secret stored and per API call.
Serverless event bus. Route events between AWS services, SaaS, and custom apps. Schedule-based or event-pattern rules. Formerly CloudWatch Events.
Web Application Firewall — protects web apps from common exploits (SQL injection, XSS). Define rules to allow/block/monitor requests. Works with CloudFront, ALB, API Gateway.
DDoS protection. Standard (free, automatic L3/L4 protection) and Advanced ($3000/mo, L7 protection, 24/7 DDoS Response Team, cost protection).
Intelligent threat detection. Analyzes CloudTrail, VPC Flow Logs, and DNS logs using ML. Detects compromised instances, reconnaissance, and account compromise.
Records and evaluates resource configurations over time. Compliance auditing. Config Rules check if resources comply with desired configurations.
Operational hub for AWS resources. Includes Session Manager (SSH-less access), Parameter Store (config/secrets), Patch Manager, Run Command, and more.
Fully managed ML platform. Build, train, and deploy machine learning models. Includes notebooks, training, tuning, and inference hosting.
Fully managed CI/CD pipeline. Automates build, test, and deploy phases. Integrates with CodeCommit, CodeBuild, CodeDeploy, and third-party tools.
Fully managed build service. Compiles code, runs tests, produces deployable artifacts. No build servers to manage. Pay per build minute.
Automated deployment service. Deploys to EC2, Fargate, Lambda, and on-premises. Supports rolling, blue/green, and canary deployments.
Elastic Container Registry — managed Docker container registry. Store, manage, and deploy container images. Integrates with ECS, EKS, and Lambda.
PaaS — deploy web apps without managing infrastructure. Supports Java, .NET, PHP, Node.js, Python, Ruby, Go, Docker. Auto handles capacity, LB, scaling.
Centralized log management. Collect, monitor, and analyze log files from EC2, Lambda, CloudTrail, and more. Set metric filters and alarms on log data.
Distributed tracing for debugging and analyzing microservices. Visualize request flow, find bottlenecks, and identify errors across services.
Managed document database compatible with MongoDB. Scales storage automatically. Supports JSON workloads with MongoDB API compatibility.
Managed graph database. Supports Apache TinkerPop Gremlin and SPARQL. Use for social networks, fraud detection, knowledge graphs, recommendation engines.
Quantum Ledger Database — immutable, cryptographically verifiable transaction log. Tracks all changes with a journal. Serverless.
Serverless time series database. 1000x faster and 1/10th the cost of relational DBs for time series. IoT, DevOps, analytics use cases.
Managed file transfer service. SFTP, FTPS, and FTP to/from S3 or EFS. No need to manage file transfer infrastructure.
Managed file systems. FSx for Windows File Server (SMB), FSx for Lustre (HPC), FSx for NetApp ONTAP, FSx for OpenZFS.
Hybrid cloud storage. On-premises access to cloud storage. Types: File Gateway (NFS/SMB), Volume Gateway (iSCSI), Tape Gateway (VTL).
Physical data transfer devices. Snowcone (8-14 TB), Snowball Edge (80 TB), Snowmobile (100 PB). For large-scale data migration when network is impractical.
ML-powered security service that discovers and protects sensitive data in S3. Identifies PII, financial data, credentials. Generates findings and alerts.
Automated vulnerability management. Scans EC2 instances, container images in ECR, and Lambda functions for software vulnerabilities and network exposure.
Central security dashboard. Aggregates findings from GuardDuty, Inspector, Macie, Firewall Manager. Runs automated compliance checks (CIS, PCI DSS).
Centrally manage multiple AWS accounts. Service Control Policies (SCPs) for governance. Consolidated billing. Organizational Units (OUs) for grouping.
Policies in AWS Organizations that define maximum permissions for member accounts. They don't grant permissions — they set boundaries. Apply to OUs or accounts.
Set up and govern a secure multi-account environment. Automates account creation with guardrails. Extends Organizations with landing zone best practices.
Managed Grafana for operational dashboards. Query and visualize data from CloudWatch, X-Ray, Prometheus, and other sources. SSO integration.
Managed GraphQL and Pub/Sub API service. Real-time data sync. Connects to DynamoDB, Lambda, HTTP, and other data sources. Offline support for mobile.
Managed message broker for Apache ActiveMQ and RabbitMQ. For migrating existing apps that use standard messaging protocols (JMS, AMQP, MQTT, STOMP).
Improves availability and performance using AWS global network. Provides static anycast IPs. Routes traffic to optimal endpoints. Instant failover.

cards.front: What is the difference between CloudFront and Global Accelerator?
cards.back: CloudFront: CDN, caches content at edge. Global Accelerator: routes to the optimal region via AWS backbone, no caching. GA is better for non-HTTP (TCP/UDP) or non-cacheable content.
Full-stack development platform. Host web/mobile apps, backend APIs, auth, storage. CI/CD built in. Supports React, Vue, Angular, Next.js, Flutter.
Simplified cloud platform for small projects. Fixed monthly pricing. Includes compute, storage, networking. Easy VPS alternative. Good for simple websites/apps.
Run AWS infrastructure on-premises. Fully managed racks in your data center. Same APIs as in the cloud. For low-latency or data residency requirements.
AWS infrastructure placed close to population centers. Single-digit millisecond latency for applications. Extension of a Region. For real-time gaming, media, ML inference.
AWS compute and storage embedded in 5G telecom provider networks. Ultra-low latency for mobile edge computing applications.
ML-based image and video analysis. Object/scene/face detection, facial comparison, text in images, content moderation, celebrity recognition, PPE detection.
ML service that extracts text, handwriting, and structured data from scanned documents. Goes beyond OCR — understands tables and forms.
NLP service. Sentiment analysis, entity recognition, key phrase extraction, language detection, topic modeling. Custom classification and entity extraction.
Automatic speech-to-text. Real-time and batch. Custom vocabulary, speaker identification, automatic language detection. Medical variant available.
Text-to-speech service. Lifelike voices in multiple languages. Neural and standard voices. SSML support. Use for accessibility, content reading, IVR.
Neural machine translation. Real-time and batch. 75+ languages. Custom terminology for domain-specific terms. Integrates with other AWS services.
Build conversational interfaces (chatbots) with voice and text. Same technology as Alexa. Supports multi-turn conversations, slots, and intents.
ML-powered recommendation engine. Real-time personalization. No ML experience needed. Use for product recommendations, content curation, personalized search.
ML-based time series forecasting. Up to 50% more accurate than traditional methods. Use for demand planning, resource planning, financial forecasting.
Centralized backup service. Automated backup policies across EC2, EBS, RDS, DynamoDB, EFS, S3, and more. Cross-region and cross-account backup.
Best practice recommendations across 5 categories: cost optimization, performance, security, fault tolerance, service limits. Some checks free, full requires Business/Enterprise support.

cards.front: What are the AWS Support plans?
cards.back: Basic (free), Developer ($29/mo), Business ($100/mo, Trusted Advisor), Enterprise On-Ramp ($5500/mo), Enterprise ($15000/mo, TAM, Concierge).

cards.front: What is the AWS Well-Architected Framework?
cards.back: Six pillars: Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, Sustainability. Guides for building secure, resilient, efficient systems.

cards.front: What is the AWS Shared Responsibility Model?
cards.back: AWS: security OF the cloud (hardware, software, networking, facilities). Customer: security IN the cloud (data, IAM, OS patching, firewall config, encryption).
Managed search service. Set up, manage, and scale a search domain. Supports 34 languages, highlighting, autocomplete, geospatial search.
Managed Elasticsearch/OpenSearch. Full-text search, log analytics, real-time application monitoring. Successor to Amazon Elasticsearch Service.
Data transfer service. Move data between on-premises and AWS (S3, EFS, FSx). Up to 10x faster than open-source tools. Automatic encryption and validation.

cards.front: What is AWS Database Migration Service (DMS)?
cards.back: Migrate databases to AWS with minimal downtime. Supports homogeneous and heterogeneous migrations. Continuous replication for ongoing sync.
Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka. Fully managed Kafka clusters. Build real-time streaming data pipelines without managing Kafka infrastructure.
Managed Apache Cassandra-compatible database. Serverless, pay-per-use. Wide-column NoSQL for high-scale applications with Cassandra Query Language (CQL) API.

cards.front: What is Amazon MemoryDB for Redis?
cards.back: Redis-compatible durable in-memory database. Multi-AZ durability with microsecond reads. Use as both a cache AND primary database.
Fully managed container app service. Deploy from source code or container image. Auto-scales, load balances. Simpler than ECS/EKS for web apps and APIs.
Managed delivery service for container and serverless applications. Platform teams define templates; developers deploy within guardrails.

cards.front: What is the difference between Security Groups and NACLs?
cards.back: Security Groups: stateful, instance-level, allow rules only, all rules evaluated. NACLs: stateless, subnet-level, allow AND deny rules, rules processed in order.
Isolated locations within a Region. Each AZ is one or more data centers with independent power, cooling, and networking. Connected via low-latency links.

cards.front: What is a VPC Peering Connection?
cards.back: Network connection between two VPCs. Traffic stays on AWS backbone. Non-transitive (A-B and B-C does not mean A-C). Cannot have overlapping CIDR blocks.
Hub-and-spoke network connectivity. Connect thousands of VPCs and on-premises networks through a single gateway. Simplifies complex peering topologies.
Private connectivity between VPCs and services without exposing traffic to the internet. Uses VPC endpoints. Keeps data on AWS network.
DynamoDB Accelerator — fully managed in-memory cache for DynamoDB. Microsecond read latency. API-compatible, no code changes needed.
Speed up S3 uploads using CloudFront edge locations. Data routed over AWS backbone from edge to S3 bucket. Extra cost per GB.
Set up a secure data lake in days. Centralized permissions. Built on top of Glue. Define fine-grained access control. Simplifies data governance.
Serverless BI (business intelligence) service. Create dashboards, visualizations, and reports. ML-powered insights. Pay-per-session pricing.
Provision, manage, and deploy SSL/TLS certificates. Free public certificates for AWS services. Auto-renewal. Integrates with CloudFront, ALB, API Gateway.
Dedicated Hardware Security Modules in the cloud. FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated. You control keys. For regulatory compliance needing dedicated hardware.

cards.front: What is the difference between KMS and CloudHSM?
cards.back: KMS: shared multi-tenant HSM, AWS manages hardware, simpler. CloudHSM: dedicated single-tenant HSM, you manage keys, FIPS 140-2 L3, higher compliance requirements.
Serverless scheduler for creating and managing one-time or recurring tasks. More flexible than CloudWatch Events scheduled rules. At-least-once delivery.
Run batch computing jobs at any scale. Automatically provisions compute resources based on job requirements. Integrates with EC2 Spot Instances for cost savings.
Deploy and manage HPC (High-Performance Computing) clusters. Supports Slurm and AWS Batch schedulers. Auto-scaling compute nodes.
Cloud-based contact center. Omnichannel (voice, chat, tasks). Pay-per-minute pricing. AI-powered with Lex, Polly, and Contact Lens for analytics.
Simple Email Service — high-scale email sending and receiving. Transactional, marketing, and bulk emails. Pay per email sent. DKIM/SPF authentication.
Visualize and manage AWS costs and usage over time. Forecast future spending. Filter by service, account, tag. Identify cost optimization opportunities.
Set custom cost and usage budgets. Alert via SNS or email when thresholds are exceeded. Can trigger actions like stopping instances.

cards.front: What is the AWS Free Tier?
cards.back: Three types: Always Free (Lambda 1M requests/mo), 12 Months Free (EC2 t2/t3.micro 750 hrs/mo), Trials (SageMaker 2 months). Applies to new accounts.
Media transcoding in the cloud. Convert media files between formats for playback on phones, tablets, browsers. Being replaced by AWS Elemental MediaConvert.
Browser-based shell environment. Pre-authenticated with console credentials. AWS CLI, Python, Node.js pre-installed. 1 GB persistent storage per Region.
Managed Desktop-as-a-Service (DaaS). Windows or Linux virtual desktops. Persistent storage. Pay monthly or hourly. Replace traditional VDI.
Create and manage approved IT service catalogs. Standardized CloudFormation templates. Enforce governance and compliance. Self-service provisioning for users.
Multi-region, multi-active replication for DynamoDB. Sub-second replication. Active-active across regions. Automatic conflict resolution.
